title: Chop-Cherry.
content:
Thou gav'st me leave to kiss,
    Thou gav'st me leave to woo;
    Thou mad'st me think, by this
    And that, thou lov'dst me too.

    But I shall ne'er forget
    How, for to make thee merry,
    Thou mad'st me chop, but yet
    Another snapp'd the cherry.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "Chop-Cherry."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
